title: Sporadanthus interruptus
intro: Sporadanthus interruptus, also known as the Australian fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant effects. The bioactive constituents of Sporadanthus interruptus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil for topical application to alleviate coughs and congestion.
taxonomy: Sporadanthus interruptus is a species of flowering plant in the family Restionaceae. It is native to New Zealand. The plant is classified within the genus Sporadanthus, which is part of the family Restionaceae. Restionaceae is a family of plants commonly known as restio or rush-like plants. Sporadanthus interruptus is a type of monocotyledonous plant.
